etymology.description: semantic 𠬜 + phonetic 棥 (fán) -> fence or pen
etymology.interpretation: The character 樊 (fán, "fence, pen") combines the phonetic element 棥 (fán) with the semantic component 𠬜, which suggests enclosure or barrier. The combination implies an enclosure or a structure used to keep animals or other items contained, thus arriving at the meaning "fence" or "pen".

examples.chinese: 我家后院有一个很高的樊(樊)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiā hòuyuàn yǒu yí gè hěn gāo de fán.
examples.english: There is a very tall fence in my backyard.

examples.chinese: 小羊在樊里跳跃(小羊在樊裏跳躍)，看起来很开心(看起來很開心)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo yáng zài fán lǐ tiàoyuè, kàn qǐlái hěn kāixīn.
examples.english: The lambs are jumping around in the pen, looking very happy.
